name: Iznájar from Rute circular — Parque Natural Sierras Subbéticas
name_original: Iznájar desde Rute circular — Parque Natural Sierras Subbéticas
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Rute traverse the hilly terrain of the Parque Natural Sierras Subbéticas, offering challenging climbs and scenic descents. The region is characterized by its natural park landscapes, including the Iznájar Reservoir, and features significant elevation changes. Road cyclists can expect routes that navigate through rural areas and past natural features. Best road cycling routes around Rute The most popular road cycling route is Climb to the Sanctuary of Our Lady of Araceli (Lucena) — circular from Rute, a 37.0 miles (59.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours to complete. This route features a significant climb to a sanctuary, offering expansive views. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Iznájar Reservoir Loop, a difficult 34.7 miles (55.8 km) path. This route circles the Iznájar Reservoir, providing views of the water and surrounding hills. Local road cyclists also love the Iznájar from Rute circular — Parque Natural Sierras Subbéticas, a 31.3 miles (50.3 km) trail leading through the Parque Natural Sierras Subbéticas, often completed in about 2 hours 44 minutes. Road cycling around Rute is defined by hilly terrain, natural parks, and reservoirs. The network offers options for different ability levels, including moderate and difficult routes. The routes in Rute are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 270 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Rute's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Rute?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes around Rute, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored these routes extensively, with more than 270 road cyclists using komoot to discover the area.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Rute?
A: Road cycling routes around Rute primarily traverse the hilly terrain of the Parque Natural Sierras Subbéticas. You can expect significant elevation changes, with challenging climbs and scenic descents through rural areas and natural park landscapes.

Q: Are there any challenging climbs for experienced road cyclists in Rute?
A: Yes, Rute offers several challenging routes with significant elevation gain. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1008815676/climb-to-the-sanctuary-of-our-lady-of-araceli-lucena-circular-from-rute" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to the Sanctuary of Our Lady of Araceli (Lucena) — circular from Rute&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 59.5 km route featuring a substantial climb to a sanctuary with expansive views. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016911324/iznajar-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iznájar Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 55.8 km path with over 1200 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Can I find any moderate road cycling routes around Rute?
A: While many routes are classified as difficult, there are moderate options available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20120595" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Mateo, Lucena – Castillo del Moral loop from Rute&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 44 km route with less elevation gain compared to the more challenging climbs.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes in Rute?
A: Road cycling routes in Rute often pass through the Parque Natural Sierras Subbéticas and offer views of the Iznájar Reservoir. Notable natural features and landmarks in the wider area include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4050527" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Río&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3374591" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poljé of the Nava de Cabra&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/893885" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabra Station&lt;/a&gt; or religious buildings such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4741508" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santo Domingo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available from Rute?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Rute are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1294252583/iznajar-from-rute-circular-parque-natural-sierras-subbeticas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iznájar from Rute circular — Parque Natural Sierras Subbéticas&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1008824436/iznajar-from-rute-parque-natural-sierras-subbeticas-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iznajar from Rute — Parque Natural Sierras Subbeticas — circular&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Rute?
A: Given the region's climate, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant weather for road cycling in Rute, with milder temperatures. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable. Winters are typically mild but can have cooler temperatures and occasional rain.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options in Rute?
A: The road cycling routes directly around Rute are predominantly rated as moderate to difficult due to the hilly terrain and significant elevation changes. While there are no routes specifically labeled 'easy' in the guide data, families with older, experienced cyclists might find some of the moderate routes manageable. For younger children or less experienced riders, it's advisable to check the route profiles carefully for elevation and distance.

Q: Is it possible to find dog-friendly cycling trails around Rute?
A: The road cycling routes around Rute are primarily designed for human cyclists on paved or well-maintained surfaces. While dogs are generally welcome in natural park areas, specific provisions or recommendations for dog-friendly road cycling trails are not typically provided. It's best to consider the safety and comfort of your dog, especially on routes with traffic or challenging terrain.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Rute?
A: Parking is generally available in and around the town of Rute, which serves as a common starting point for many routes. For specific trailheads within the Parque Natural Sierras Subbéticas or near the Iznájar Reservoir, local parking areas or roadside pull-offs may be available. It's always recommended to check local signage for parking regulations.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or cafes along the road cycling routes?
A: Many road cycling routes around Rute pass through or near small towns and villages where you can find cafes, bars, and shops for refreshments and supplies. Routes that circle the Iznájar Reservoir or head towards Lucena will offer opportunities for stops. Planning your route with komoot allows you to identify potential points of interest for breaks.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Rute?
A: The road cycling experience in Rute is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Cyclists often praise the challenging climbs, the stunning views over the Parque Natural Sierras Subbéticas and the Iznájar Reservoir, and the quiet, rural roads that allow for an immersive experience in nature.
tips: From Puente Fernández we can see the Genil River that feeds the Iznájar Reservoir and part of the old Molinillo bridge, which is covered when the water level is high.

Iznájar's location on a small cape surrounded by water makes it a place of great scenic value. The entire town has infinite viewpoints that make us enjoy the views of the Genil River and the reservoir. Passing through Iznájar, it is worth getting lost in its narrow streets and, above all, seeing the Patio de las Comedias, full of blue pots.
